title: Form with embedded schema for UI-specific validation
prompt: Write two files: (1) lib/my_app/checkout/form.ex (module MyApp.Checkout.Form) as an Ecto.Schema with `use Ecto.Schema`, `import Ecto.Changeset`, an embedded_schema declaring fields :cardholder, :card_last4, :zipcode, and a changeset/2 that casts + validates them. (2) lib/my_app_web/live/checkout_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.CheckoutLive) that uses MyApp.Checkout.Form (NOT the DB schema MyApp.Checkout.Order directly) for the <.form>. mount builds @form via to_form(Form.changeset(%Form{}, %{})). Decoupling the UI form from the DB schema is the key pattern.
tier_rationale: Requires introducing a separate form-specific schema — an architectural decision many LLMs miss.
research_citation: John Elm Labs top-3 form mistakes #2: 'Brittle system where UI and database can\'t evolve independently'
